edge_hypothesis: Williams %R and STIFFNESS are two indicator families with ZERO representation in any ACCEPT across 706+ outcomes. This spec tests them TOGETHER as a mean-reversion system. The thesis: Williams %R (oscillator from -100 to 0) at extreme readings (<-80 oversold, >-20 overbought) signals that price has stretched beyond its recent range. STIFFNESS measures how rigidly price follows its SMA — LOW stiffness (< 50) means price is NOT trending (it's wobbling around the mean), confirming mean-reversion conditions. This is a NOVEL regime filter: where CHOP measures bar-level choppiness, STIFFNESS measures price-to-SMA adherence. A mean-reversion signal (WILLR extreme) paired with a mean-reversion regime filter (low STIFFNESS) should COMPOUND: both independently indicate that price will revert, and together they should filter out false signals where WILLR is extreme but a genuine trend is in progress. KEY DIFFERENCE from CCI spec: WILLR is bounded (-100 to 0) while CCI is unbounded. WILLR extremes are more clearly defined. STIFFNESS is a completely different regime filter from CHOP — if one works and the other doesn't, we learn something about which regime detection method suits mean-reversion strategies. THREE OUTCOMES: (1) PF > 1.2 with 40+ trades: WILLR + STIFFNESS is a viable new signal family. (2) PF 0.8-1.2: marginal — WILLR signals have some predictive power but STIFFNESS filtering needs calibration. (3) PF < 0.8: WILLR extremes don't predict reversion direction OR STIFFNESS threshold is wrong. INVALIDATION: If both WILLR specs produce PF < 0.7, Williams %R is not a useful entry signal for crypto mean-reversion.

variants.name: willr_stiffness_fade_1h_8to1
variants.description: Williams %R at extremes with STIFFNESS regime gate on ETH/1h. Entry when WILLR hits oversold/overbought in a non-stiff (mean-reverting) market. 8:1 R:R. First-ever test of BOTH Williams %R and STIFFNESS in any strategy. WILLR < -80 = oversold (price at bottom of recent range). STIFFNESS < 50 = price is NOT adhering to trend (likely to revert). Expected: 40-100 trades.
filters: STIFFNESS regime gate: STIFFNESS_20_3_100 < 50 = price not trending
Novel regime filter: STIFFNESS measures SMA adherence, not bar choppiness
First-ever test of WILLR and STIFFNESS indicators
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: WILLR_14 at extremes (<-80 / >-20). First-ever Williams %R test.
STIFFNESS_20_3_100 < 50 = price not trending. Novel regime filter.

variants.name: willr_stiffness_fade_4h_8to1
variants.description: Williams %R + STIFFNESS fade on ETH/4h. Same thesis on higher timeframe. 4h WILLR extremes represent larger-scale oversold/overbought conditions. Expected: 15-40 trades. Diagnostic: does 4h produce higher PF than 1h for this signal type?
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 8:1. Needs 11.1% win rate to break even after fees.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: WILLR_14 extremes on 4h — larger-scale reversion signals.

variants.name: willr_stiffness_fade_1h_tight_5to1
variants.description: Williams %R + STIFFNESS with TIGHTER WILLR bands (-90/-10) and 5:1 R:R. The HIGH-CONVICTION variant. WILLR < -90 means price is at the EXTREME bottom of its recent range (bottom 10%). This should fire less often but with higher reversion probability. Combined with STIFFNESS < 40 (even less trending), this is the strictest possible mean-reversion filter using these two indicators. 5:1 R:R (1.5x / 7.5x) captures shorter reversion moves. Expected: 15-40 trades. If PF > 1.3 despite fewer trades: extreme WILLR readings PLUS strict STIFFNESS produce very high-quality signals. Compare to base variant to measure conviction improvement.
filters: High-conviction variant: strictest filters, fewer but higher-quality signals
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 5:1. Needs 16.7% win rate to break even after fees.
Shorter TP targets moderate reversion moves from extreme WILLR readings.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.5x ATR_14 from entry.
